title: FileIndex paths and lower_paths are two parallel Vecs invariant must be maintained by hand
description:
## Severity
Minor

## Dimension
Type Safety / Data Modeling

## Location
src/tools/file_index.rs:28-32 (FileIndex struct), 72-76 (walk_dir push), 89-104 (search zip)

## Violation
```rust
pub struct FileIndex {
    paths: Vec<String>,
    lower_paths: Vec<String>,
}
```

Two parallel Vecs that MUST stay index-synchronized. Every push to `paths` MUST also push to `lower_paths`; every read from one MUST zip with the other. The invariant is enforced only by the convention that walk_dir does both pushes (line 74-75). Search uses `self.paths.iter().zip(self.lower_paths.iter())` (line 91). If a future maintainer adds a path to one Vec but not the other (e.g. in a hypothetical `remove` method, or during incremental updates), index returns drift silently — search would skip files or score the wrong file.

## Mandated refactor
```rust
struct IndexedPath { display: String, lower: String }
pub struct FileIndex { paths: Vec<IndexedPath> }
```
One push per file, one iteration per search. The invariant is enforced by the type system. Performance is identical (struct of strings = same allocation pattern as paired Vecs).

## Filed from QA review dispatch
